did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper reports on specific AI/ML applications in dermatology, including a pilot study using weakly supervised models to differentiate early mycosis fungoides (MF) from benign inflammatory dermatoses (BIDs) with 75.9% accuracy, and a study by Zheng et al. using a ResNet-34 convolutional neural network (CNN) to detect neoplastic cells in primary cutaneous CD30-positive lymphoproliferative disorders. It also discusses AI applications for basal cell carcinoma (BCC) detection and melanocytic lesion classification.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The title explicitly states 'A review of the current state and future perspectives'. The abstract states 'This review summarizes and blends applications of AI in pathology of skin cancer and lymphoma'. The introduction mentions 'In this review, we discuss how a synergy between previous works... could be adapted'. It synthesizes existing literature rather than presenting original experimental results as its primary contribution.

non_compliance_reason: The paper is a review, survey, or meta-analysis rather than an original research paper.
